supported_examples.claim: Type 2 diabetes is a chronic metabolic disorder characterized by insulin resistance [1].
Metformin is the first-line pharmacological treatment for type 2 diabetes [2].
It reduces HbA1c levels by approximately 1-2% [3].
Recent studies have shown potential cardiovascular benefits beyond glycemic control [4][5].
Metformin works primarily by decreasing hepatic glucose production [6].

unsupported_examples.claim: The medication is generally well-tolerated by most patients.
It also improves insulin sensitivity in peripheral tissues.
The exact molecular mechanisms are still being investigated.
